description: Before I knew what cybersecurity was, I was taking things apart to see how they broke. That instinct — find the weak point, understand the system — is what pulled me away from civil and systems engineering and straight into security.

I studied Software Systems Engineering at the University of Regina, Canada. Alongside coursework, I worked safety compliance at Co-op Refinery Complex — where a missed check could cost lives. That environment taught me something no classroom does: attention to detail isn't optional when the stakes are high. That mindset transferred directly to security.

I completed a Network Security Engineering internship at ProSec Telecommunications in Canada, deploying Cisco firewalls, IPsec VPNs, EIGRP/OSPF/BGP routing, and SIEM solutions on live enterprise infrastructure. I hold AZ-500, CompTIA Security+, CompTIA Network+, and Palo Alto certifications. Outside work, I build home labs — Wazuh SIEM, pfSense, Azure security stacks — and compete on Hack The Box and TryHackMe. Authorized to work in Canada until 2028.
